+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-188
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-25_23-12-35+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 — removed the two steady-state 5s primary-queue
+  retry sleeps after `auto.queue-check: primary done`; an empty primary result
+  now falls through immediately to `Get-OpenQueueIssuesRest`.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_TASK_2026-05-25_23-06-13+0300.md — source TASK packet
+  for this dispatch.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-12-35+0300.md — this execution report.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_TASK_2026-05-25_23-06-13+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`: in the queue-presence block right after
+  `Write-TimingTrace "auto.queue-check: primary done (count=...)"`, removed the
+  `for ($poll = 1; $poll -le 2 -and $openQueue.Count -eq 0; $poll++)` retry
+  loop that performed two five-second `Start-Sleep` waits and re-ran the
+  primary `gh issue list` query. Also removed the now-redundant nested
+  `if ($openQueue.Count -eq 0) { ... }` wrapper that previously gated the REST
+  fallback behind the retry loop. The REST issues cross-check
+  (`Get-OpenQueueIssuesRest`) now runs immediately when the first primary
+  query returns zero. Updated the adjacent comment so it accurately describes
+  the immediate REST fallback instead of a primary-query retry.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added. The Task Packet explicitly forbids adding tests, fixtures, or
+  any new file outside this dispatch's own allowed handoff artifacts.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None added. The Task Packet explicitly forbids docs, ADRs, or status-file
+  edits in this dispatch.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Single source edit in `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`:
+
+- Behaviour preserved:
+  - Primary query returning `> 0` → unchanged: report pending queue, drain it,
+    skip cap check and selection.
+  - Primary returns `0`, REST finds queued issues → unchanged messages, REST
+    items mapped into `$openQueue`, cap check skipped, queue drained.
+  - Primary returns `0`, REST returns `0` → unchanged message
+    ("REST issues check confirms no open '$queueLabel' issues."), proceed to
+    cap check.
+  - Primary returns `0`, REST fails → unchanged warning, `$queueStateAmbiguous
+    = $true`, tick exits 0 without filing work or invoking the queue.
+  - Post-issue-creation visibility wait (the `for ($poll = 1; $poll -le 12;
+    $poll++) { Start-Sleep -Seconds 5; ... }` block after `gh issue create`)
+    is untouched.
+  - `Write-TimingTrace` call sites, event names, message strings, JSONL
+    schema/path/enablement, and trace failure behaviour are unchanged.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+  (`[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile(...)`):
+  exit 0, output `PARSE OK`, zero parser errors.
+- `git diff --check`: exit 0 (no whitespace/conflict markers introduced).
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1`: exit 0. Final line: `VERIFY OK: all 7
+  verification step(s) passed.` All seven steps green:
+  1. `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check`,
+  2. `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` (10 enforcement
+     lints PASS + snapshot-participate supplementary 3/3),
+  3. `cargo test -p rge-tool-architecture-lints --all-targets` (38 tests
+     passed),
+  4. workspace builds/tests as configured by the gate script,
+  5. workspace lib/integration tests,
+  6. `cargo test --workspace --doc`,
+  7. `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`.
+- Final diff scope inspection (`git status --short`, `git diff --stat`,
+  `git diff Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`): the only source-tree edit is
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` (+20 / -32). Untracked files in `ai_handoffs/`
+  are only this dispatch's own `ISSUE-188_TASK_*` packet, its sidecar, and
+  this `ISSUE-188_EXEC_*` packet (sidecar finalized below). No queue-log
+  artifact was created by the queue runner (the queue was not invoked during
+  verification, per scope rules). No edits outside the allowed envelope.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. Only
+`Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` was modified, the empty-queue retry loop was
+removed (not replaced with a zero-delay retry), REST fallback semantics and
+the post-issue-creation visibility wait are preserved, trace surface is
+unchanged, and the only added files are this dispatch's own EXEC handoff
+artifacts.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+None. The change is the minimal localised edit specified in the Planner Notes:
+delete the two-sleep retry loop and let the existing REST cross-check run as
+the immediate fallback. Comment text near the edited block was lightly
+rewritten so it no longer claims a primary-query retry happens; per the
+Deliverables this comment update is explicitly allowed.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` is the only modified-but-
+  uncommitted file (no commit performed, per protocol).
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_TASK_2026-05-25_23-06-13+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_TASK_2026-05-25_23-06-13+0300.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-12-35+0300.md` (this packet; its
+  `.meta.json` sidecar is produced by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`).
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-188`
+- Last commit: `e2cf3dc chore(dispatch): queue empty tick speedup`
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-188
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,251 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-188
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-25_23-06-13+0300
+REVISION: 0
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_TASK_2026-05-25_23-06-13+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_TASK_2026-05-25_23-06-13+0300.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_CORRECT_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_CORRECT_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+- GitHub issue #188
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Remove the empty-tick steady-state delay from `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` for
+GitHub issue #188. The auto driver currently performs two five-second primary
+queue-query retries when the initial `ai-dispatch` queue check returns zero
+before it reaches the cap check. This dispatch must eliminate those two
+steady-state sleeps so an empty queue falls through immediately to the existing
+REST issues cross-check, while preserving the safety behavior that prevents
+new work selection or cap-halting on ambiguous queue state.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+- This dispatch's own generated handoff artifacts only:
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_TASK_2026-05-25_23-06-13+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_TASK_2026-05-25_23-06-13+0300.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_EXEC_*.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_EXEC_*.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_CORRECT_*.md`, and
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_CORRECT_*.meta.json`.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one. Do not manually create or edit unrelated dispatch logs.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `new-handoff.ps1`
+- `.ai/**`, including `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`,
+  `.ai/codex_control.schema.json`, `.ai/handoff.schema.json`, run-dir control
+  JSON shapes, dashboards, schemas, prompts, and task-selection briefs
+- `ai_handoffs/**`, except this dispatch's own `ISSUE-188_TASK`,
+  `ISSUE-188_EXEC`, and `ISSUE-188_CORRECT` Markdown packets and matching
+  `.meta.json` sidecars listed above
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/**`, except this dispatch's own exact queue log artifact if
+  the queue runner creates one
+- Queue runner behavior, loop runner behavior, watchdog behavior, preflight
+  checks, scheduled-task registration, GitHub labels, retry policy outside the
+  targeted empty-queue retry removal, recovery routes, publish policy, branch
+  policy, halt sentinel behavior, issue-selection prompt behavior, Rust source,
+  Cargo manifests, `Cargo.lock`, tests, fixtures, workflows, docs, status
+  files, architecture-lint files, `.gitignore`, dashboards, or repository
+  metadata
+
+### MAY add new files
+
+- This dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_EXEC_*.md` execution
+  report and matching generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+  sidecar.
+- This dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_CORRECT_*.md`
+  correction packet and matching generated
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_CORRECT_*.meta.json` sidecar, only if a correction
+  round is required by the orchestrator.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+
+- Any new source file, test file, fixture, generated asset, manifest, lockfile,
+  dependency file, crate, workflow, schema, script, doc, ADR, plan, status file,
+  architecture-lint artifact, dashboard, `.gitignore` entry, or repository
+  metadata file.
+- Any handoff packet or sidecar for another dispatch.
+- Any `REVIEW` or `CLOSEOUT` packet for this orchestrator dispatch.
+- Any `ai_dispatch_logs/**` file other than this dispatch's own exact queue log
+  artifact if the queue runner creates one.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- In `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, remove the two steady-state five-second
+  queue-check sleeps that run when the first primary open `ai-dispatch` queue
+  query returns zero before the autonomous task cap check.
+- Remove the associated two-attempt primary queue retry loop rather than
+  replacing the sleeps with a zero-delay or shorter-delay retry. After the
+  primary queue query reports zero, the existing REST issues cross-check must
+  run immediately as the fallback.
+- Keep `Get-OpenQueueIssuesRest` as the immediate fallback for a zero-count
+  primary queue query. The fallback must still:
+  - convert REST-visible queued issues into `$openQueue` entries so queued work
+    drains before the cap check,
+  - print the existing REST-visible queued-work message when REST finds open
+    queue issues,
+  - print the existing REST-confirms-empty message when REST succeeds and finds
+    no queue issues, and
+  - mark queue state ambiguous and skip the tick when REST fails.
+- Preserve queued-work-before-cap semantics: any open queued `ai-dispatch`
+  issue found by either the primary query or the REST fallback must be drained
+  by running `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` before any autonomous task cap check
+  can halt or any new issue can be selected.
+- Preserve ambiguous-queue skip behavior: when the primary query returns zero
+  and the REST fallback fails, the tick must still exit 0 after printing the
+  existing ambiguous-queue messages, without selecting new work, without
+  running the cap check, and without invoking the queue.
+- Preserve the post-issue-creation visibility wait loop unchanged. The loop
+  that sleeps up to twelve times after `gh issue create` so a newly filed issue
+  becomes label-listable must remain functionally and textually unchanged
+  except for unavoidable surrounding whitespace from this dispatch's targeted
+  edit.
+- Preserve existing console trace and JSONL trace behavior. Do not add,
+  remove, rename, reorder, or reshape `Write-TimingTrace` events, and do not
+  change the JSONL trace schema, trace file path policy, trace enablement
+  policy, or trace failure behavior.
+- It is acceptable to update only the nearby source comment that currently
+  describes retrying the primary query, so the comment accurately says the
+  empty primary result falls through to the REST issues cross-check.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only source file changed is `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`.
+- The empty-queue path no longer waits ten seconds before the cap check: there
+  is no two-iteration `Start-Sleep -Seconds 5` retry loop between
+  `Write-TimingTrace "auto.queue-check: primary done (...)"` and the REST
+  issues cross-check.
+- When the first primary queue query returns one or more open `ai-dispatch`
+  issues, behavior is unchanged: the auto driver reports the pending queue and
+  proceeds to drain it without running the cap check or selecting new work.
+- When the primary queue query returns zero and the REST issues fallback finds
+  queued work, behavior is unchanged except for the removed steady-state wait:
+  the auto driver reports the REST-visible queue, populates `$openQueue`, skips
+  the cap check, and drains the queue.
+- When the primary queue query returns zero and the REST issues fallback
+  confirms zero queued issues, the auto driver proceeds to the existing
+  autonomous task cap check immediately.
+- When the primary queue query returns zero and the REST issues fallback fails,
+  behavior is unchanged except for the removed steady-state wait: the auto
+  driver marks queue state ambiguous, exits 0 after the existing skip messages,
+  and does not run the cap check, select work, create an issue, or invoke the
+  queue.
+- The issue-creation visibility wait loop after `gh issue create` still sleeps
+  in five-second increments for up to twelve polls and retains its existing
+  success and warning output.
+- Existing `Write-TimingTrace` call sites, event names, message strings, JSONL
+  field names, JSONL field types, and trace enablement behavior are unchanged.
+- No queue runner, loop runner, watchdog, preflight, scheduler, label, retry
+  policy outside the targeted empty-queue retry removal, recovery route, Rust,
+  Cargo, documentation, schema, dashboard, workflow, or test behavior changes.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This dispatch is only for removing the steady-state empty-queue primary retry
+  sleeps from `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`.
+- Do not change the REST fallback into an optional or delayed check. It must be
+  the immediate fallback when the primary queue query returns zero.
+- Do not use this dispatch to tune the newly created issue visibility wait,
+  queue invocation, cap policy, task selection prompt, halt sentinel, publish
+  policy, branch policy, retries in other scripts, or GitHub label management.
+- Do not add configuration switches, environment variables, logs, dashboards,
+  schemas, docs, tests, dependencies, helper scripts, or new trace events.
+- Do not run a full autonomous dispatch or queue dispatch solely to verify this
+  timing cleanup; avoid GitHub issue creation, label mutation, branch mutation,
+  commit, push, or publish side effects during verification.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
+`EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, expected exit
+  0:
+
+```powershell
+$tokens = $null
+$errors = $null
+[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile(
+  (Resolve-Path '.\Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1'),
+  [ref]$tokens,
+  [ref]$errors
+) | Out-Null
+if ($errors.Count -ne 0) {
+  $errors | Format-List *
+  exit 1
+}
+```
+
+- `git diff --check` is expected to exit 0.
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` is expected to exit 0 and report `VERIFY OK`.
+- Inspect the final diff and document that changed files are limited to
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, this dispatch's own allowed handoff artifacts,
+  and this dispatch's own exact queue-log artifact if one exists.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of
+the following occur:
+
+- The requested cleanup cannot be implemented by editing only
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` plus this dispatch's own generated artifacts.
+- Removing the steady-state sleeps would also require changing the
+  post-issue-creation visibility wait loop.
+- Preserving the REST issues cross-check as the immediate zero-primary fallback
+  conflicts with removing the primary retry loop.
+- Queued-work-before-cap semantics cannot be preserved.
+- Ambiguous-queue skip behavior cannot be preserved.
+- Existing console trace or JSONL trace behavior would need to change,
+  including trace schema, event names, message strings, enablement, file path
+  policy, or best-effort failure behavior.
+- The work appears to require changes to queue runner, loop runner, watchdog,
+  preflight, scheduler, labels, retry policy outside the targeted empty-queue
+  retry removal, recovery routes, Rust, Cargo, docs, schemas, dashboards,
+  workflows, tests, dependencies, or any file outside the allowed edit set.
+- PowerShell parser validation, `git diff --check`, or
